task_completion.description: The CT Cranium DICOM scan is visible in the InVesalius 3 interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius 3 is open and displays axial, coronal, and sagittal views of a skull/cranium CT scan.

task_completion.description: A bone segmentation mask is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored overlay (e.g., green or yellow) is visible on the 2D CT slices, indicating that the bone structure has been segmented.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface reconstruction of the skull is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of the skull is visible in the 3D viewer pane (usually the bottom right quadrant) of the InVesalius interface.

task_completion.description: The 3D surface is exported as a Wavefront OBJ file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog for exporting 3D surfaces shows the path '/home/ga/Documents/skull_surface.obj', or a file manager window shows 'skull_surface.obj' in the Documents folder.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the surgical view is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog for exporting a picture shows the path '/home/ga/Documents/surgical_view.png', or a file manager window shows 'surgical_view.png' in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with InVesalius menus, buttons, and dialogs, rather than terminal commands or scripts being used to generate mock files.

integrity.description: The 3D surface reconstruction is genuinely generated from the DICOM data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D model in the viewer should visually match the bone structure seen in the 2D slices, rather than appearing as a generic or unrelated imported 3D model.

integrity.description: The screenshot is exported using the application's built-in functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use InVesalius's 'Save Picture' or similar export feature, rather than just taking a system-level screenshot and renaming it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the CT Cranium DICOM dataset (case 0051). The Wavefront OBJ model must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/skull_surface.obj. The surgical view PNG screenshot must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/surgical_view.png.
